title: Crispy Thai Pork Pilaf
description: An earthy and festive Thai dish featuring pork chops caramelized in a nut-free-friendly crispy sauce.

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.notes: use a microplane for finer texture

ingredients.name: red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: optional, add for extra color and crunch

ingredients.name: rice vermicelli
ingredients.amount: 4 ounces, soaked in hot water until soft

instructions: Prepare pork chops by coating them with a light layer of soy sauce and letting them marinate for at least 30 minutes or up to overnight.
Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic, finely chopped shallots, and grated ginger; sauté until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes.

Add diced red bell pepper if using, and cook for another minute. Pour in the soy sauce and lime juice, stir to combine, then add marinated pork chops, skin-side down. Cook for 6-8 minutes per side or until golden-brown on both sides.
Meanwhile, prepare rice vermicelli by adding hot water to them in a medium-sized pot and simmering until tender but still firm. Drain well and set aside.

To finish the dish, remove pork chops from skillet and place over low heat; add rice vermicelli into the skillet along with a tablespoon of oil. Cook for about 2-3 minutes stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Serve the crispy Thai Pork Pilaf topped with fresh scallions as garnish.
